content.assessment: This proposal most directly addresses the implementation-planning gaps identified across the evidence base: it proposes formal assessment procedures, transport guarantees, consultation rights, and non-discrimination catchment review — essentially building the missing feedback, safeguard, and monitoring architecture into the closure mechanism itself. However, its delivery still depends on the same centralized authority (Klebelsberg Center) enacting and enforcing these procedures, and on resolving a documented decade-long enforcement gap in segregation-related legal safeguards, which raises serious doubt about whether stated guarantees would be operationalized rather than symbolic.
opportunities: Given ageing/attrition teacher workforce, formal closure procedures could be paired with transfer planning under the 2023 Status Law provisions for involuntary transfers, potentially easing staffing at consolidated sites (F-live-implementation_planning-08)
strengths: Explicitly proposes resolving the legal ambiguity in transport-provision responsibility via statute, directly targeting a documented implementation gap (F-live-implementation_planning-09 partially, ombudsman finding)
Non-discrimination catchment review requirement responds directly to the enforcement gap in existing segregation safeguards (F-live-implementation_planning-07)
Builds in formal consultation procedure, addressing the gap left by post-2010 curtailment of stakeholder mechanisms (F-live-implementation_planning-10)
threats: A statutory procedure administered by the same centralized actor without a track record of enforcing existing legal safeguards risks becoming a procedural formality rather than a substantive safeguard (F-live-implementation_planning-01, F-live-implementation_planning-07)
Transport-guarantee costs could be undermined by pre-existing frequency gaps concentrated in the most depopulating regions (F-live-implementation_planning-04, F-live-implementation_planning-09)
weaknesses: Even with new statutory guarantees, transport frequency gaps remain unaddressed in exactly the regions with highest depopulation, meaning the mitigation infrastructure may not reach the areas most affected by closures (F-live-implementation_planning-04, F-live-implementation_planning-09)

ECtHR and Committee of Ministers monitoring on segregation has not achieved enforcement for over a decade, casting doubt on whether a domestically legislated catchment-review safeguard would be effectively enforced (F-live-implementation_planning-07)
